observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:

Municipal Engineer and/or other Officials concerned prepare turnover documents as basis for the derecognition of assets which were already transferred to the recipient agencies; and 

Municipal Accountant record adjustments corresponding to the noted accounting errors based on relevant and sufficient documents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the General Services Officer to prioritize the registration and transfer of title of the subject parcels of land to ensure ownership and to fully protect the interest of the Municipality in compliance with Section 39 of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that Management expedite the implementation of projects funded under the LGSF-SBDP in order to address the development backlog in areas that were previously affected by conflict, ensuring these communities have access to essential services and infrastructures.

We recommended that the Municipal School Board and the Municipal Accountant diligently comply with the reportorial requirements in the allocation and utilization on Special Education Fund as outlined in the aforementioned regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:

Municipal Accountant to return to the SAs the unutilized/unused funds for completed PPAs and submit liquidation reports to close the account immediately, after all obligations related to a particular PPA have been paid/settled.

We recommended that Management adhere with the submission of the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation duly signed by the Local Chief Executive, within sixty (60) days from the date of receipt of the COA Annual Audit Reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Assessor to come up with the complete of List of Taxpayers per Assessed Real Property Unit to support the amount of yearend RPT/SET Receivables reported in the financial statements.

At the beginning of every year, the Municipal Accountant shall set up the RPT/SET Receivables based on the correct Certified List of taxpayers showing the amount of tax due and collectible from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office pursuant to Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.

The Municipal Accountant shall make the necessary adjustments, based on valid  supporting documents, to correct the the RPT/SET Receivables account in the financial statements, if found misstated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant and other department heads concerned, to verify the status of these funds from its source agencies and determine if these are still recorded as liability of the Municipality in the former’s books of accounts. If so, liquidation of the said liability should be made outright by submitting to the source agencies the necessary supporting documents and returning the balances thereof, duly acknowledged by the source agency.

We further recommended that these funds should be remitted to the Bureau of Treasury if these are not recorded anymore in the books of the identified source NGAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Internal Audit Unit of the LGU to handle internal control functions within the agency pursuant to DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-5, DBM Circular Letter No. 2008-8 and COA Circular No. 2009-004.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and government officials and employees concerned refund the excess amount of P89,377.25, the add on fees for meals of P11,606.00 and the excessive hotel accommodation and meals of P20,492.00 during the ELA conducted in Taguig City.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor, fill up the vacant positions in the MDRRM Office to guarantee the adequacy of personnel who will assist in the preparation, implementation and coordination of DRRM plans and programs and enhance the capacity of the Municipality before, during and after the occurrence of disasters.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and officials/employees concerned to refund the amount of P494,000.00 immediately. We also recommended that the utilization of the 20% EDF funds be made only for expenditures expressly stated in the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 to avoid disallowance of transactions in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the recipient barangays to submit the Fund Utilization Reports to the Municipal Accountant and/or return the unutilized funds to the Municipal Treasurer.

Also, to communicate with the DSWD Regional Office No. VIII requesting for confirmation whether all KALAHI-CIDSS projects implemented in selected barangays of the Municipality were already completed as basis of the Accountant to reduce the receivables accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:

Municipal Accountant to return to the SAs the unutilized/unused funds for completed PPAs and submit liquidation reports to close the account immediately, after all obligations related to a particular PPA have been paid/settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and government officials and employees concerned refund the excess amount of P89,377.25, the add on fees for meals of P11,606.00 and the excessive hotel accommodation and meals of P20,492.00 during the ELA conducted in Taguig City.

1.7 We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Engineer and/or other Officials concerned prepare turnover
documents as basis for the derecognition of assets which were already
transferred to the recipient agencies; and
b. Municipal Accountant record adjustments corresponding to the noted
accounting errors based on relevant and sufficient documents.

2.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
DRRM Council, MDRRMO and Municipal Budget Office to:
a. equitably allocate the 70% LDRRMF for disaster prevention and
mitigation, preparedness, response, and rehabilitation and recovery;
b. ensure that the proposed LDRRMFIP and Annual Budget specifically
identify or itemize the PPAs to be funded under the 70% LDRRMF;
c. set definite and realistic timelines of PPAs’ implementation in the
proposed LDRRMFIP; and
d. adhere to the provisions of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2014-01 dated April 4, 2014 and other
regulations on LDRRMF utilization.

3.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
a. concerned officials to fast tract the implementation of PPAs covered by
these fund transfers in order to accomplish the purpose for which the
funds were granted and deliver immediate and optimum result to target
beneficiaries; and
b. Municipal Accountant to return to the SAs the unutilized/unused funds
for completed PPAs and submit liquidation reports to close the account
immediately, after all obligations related to a particular PPA have been
paid/settled.
3.9 Likewise, for subsequent fund transfer from NGAs and other government
institutions, we recommended that Management require the MPDO or the
Project Management Office to implement PPAs in accordance with the
timelines as provided in the MOA or within the project duration as stated in
the budget proposal, in the absence of MOA.

4.5 We recommended that management:
a. fully utilize the automated teller machine payroll system for payments of
employees’ bonuses, allowances and other benefits; and
b. discontinue the practice of paying expenses out of the cash advances when
they could be practically paid through checks in compliance with COA
Circular No. 97-002.

5.5 We recommended that management undertake the procedures prescribed
under Sections 254 and 256 of RA 7160 relative to the enforcement of
collection of real property taxes, to lessen the accumulation of tax
delinquencies. Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer to adopt measures
and strategies to improve collection efficiency such as vigorous information
campaign addressed to the taxpayers and deputizing the barangay treasurers
to collect all taxes on real property located in the barangay, as provided
under Section 247, Chapter VI of RA 7160.
5.6 Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to remit the
respective shares of the barangays on the real property tax collected within
five (5) days after the end of each quarter in compliance with Section 286 of
RA 7160.

6.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require concerned officials
to ensure that the performance bond for infrastructure projects that will be
posted by the winning bidder covers from the date of the commencement of
the project until after one year from the date of completion of the project in
order to adequately safeguard the interest of the Municipality.

7.4 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer verify the Official Receipt
issued by contractor/supplier to acknowledge payment of infrastructure
projects/goods/services and ensure that the amount reflected therein is the
gross payment made as required in Section 113 of RA No. 8424, before
releasing the check to said contractor/supplier.

8.6 We recommended that Management adhere with the submission of the
Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation duly signed by the Local
Chief Executive, within sixty (60) days from the date of receipt of the COA
Annual Audit Reports.
8.7 Management was amenable to the audit observation and agreed to comply with
the audit recommendation.

1.14 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. the Municipal Accountant to prepare immediately the appropriate journal
entries to record the adjustments for the stale checks and the amount
credited by the bank in favor of the agency through credit memos to afford
accuracy of the Cash and Cash Equivalent account in the Statement of
Financial Positions as of yearend, eventually correcting the
understatements of the Cash, Accounts Payable and Trust Liability
accounts by P167,965.89, P33,514.70 and P134,451.19, respectively;
b. the Municipal Treasurer to give notice to the payees concerned at least one-
month before the checks become stale, and in case of replacing stale checks,
report the cancelled check at the period of cancellation and the
replacement check chronologically in the RCI.
1.15 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert effort to obtain
copy of the MOA for the following:
a) DAR-ARCP II Project Operations Maintenance (OPEX);
b) SEDIP;
c) GTZ EFOS;
d) DOE; and
e) RAY, and adhere to the stipulations contained therein as to
unutilized funds and take appropriate actions thereon;
1.16 Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, shall take into
considerations the stipulations contained in the MOA with the respective NGA
concerned for the above stated Trust Fund Bank accounts and order the closure of
the unnecessary dormant bank accounts and revert the funds to the General Fund or
return to the source agency, whichever is appropriate.

2.13 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services
Officer to:
a. conduct physical count of all 11 Inventory accounts as of May 31, 2022 and
prepare a Report of Physical Count of Inventories which shall be submitted to
the Municipal Accountant, copy furnished the Audit Team.
b. prepare and maintain the Stock Card for each type of item to monitor the
movement of the supplies. Record all procurements based on the
Inspection and Acceptance Report (IAR) and issuances based on the
Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) duly supported with
Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS).
c. prepare and submit the required SSMI duly supported with the RIS every
month to the MACCO for recording in the books to recognize the expenses
for the month;
2.14 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. reconcile accounting records as of May 31, 2022 with the RPCI submitted
by the GSO and investigate differences, if there’s any, and prepare
adjusting entries to bring the accounts in order; and
b. calculate cost of inventory on the basis of weighted average on the date of
issue in compliance with Section 115 of the MNGAS Vol. 1.

3.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee
who will conduct a complete physical count of all PPEs per approved Physical
Inventory Plan (PIP), complete the inventory taking activities, prepare and
submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE) on or before August 31, 2022 to afford reconciliation of PPEs
before the year ends.
3.13 We also recommended for the Municipal Accountant to:
a) conduct a reconciliation of the PPEs included in the RPCPPE with the total
PPE per schedule recorded in the books of accounts of the Municipality in
coordination with the Municipal General Services Officer (MGS Officer);
b) prepare the necessary report on the results of the reconciliation to the
Municipal Mayor for his appropriate action, in cases of non-
existing/missing properties, if any; and
c) prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to recognize appropriate
depreciation expenses for:
1) 1 unit road concreting project of P1,096,343.85 acquired May 16,
2019; and
2) 1 unit front desk of P59,950.00 acquired July 27, 2021.
d) correct the following erroneous recordings in the PPE account:
i. negative accumulated depreciation of one unit air conditioner of
P12,801.25;
ii. Payroll amounting to P20,000.00 erroneously recorded as Other
Land Improvement.
3.14 Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the
Municipal Accountant and MGS Officer, perform the procedures to account
for non-existing/missing properties as a result of the reconciliation conducted
to facilitate the disposition of these items in accordance with the provisions of
COA Circular No. 2020-006 to reflect a reliable balance of the related PPE
accounts in the Financial Statement.
3.15 Lastly, we recommended that both the Accounting and the Property Unit
maintain an updated and reconciled PPELCs and PCs for each type of PPE,
respectively.

4.14 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairperson of the Municipal
DRRM Council and the Municipal DRRM Officer implement the remaining
unimplemented PPAs; and
4.15 Henceforth, we recommended that the MDRRM Council evaluate the PPAs
incorporated in the Municipal DRRM Plan and identify those projects that
could enhance the capacity of the LGU to respond to disaster and to address
the current state of calamity or any calamity that may possibly arise, and have
these projects a priority for implementation.

5.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the members of
the Municipal Planning and Development Council ensure that only those
projects which are well-planned and procurement – and – implementation-
ready are included in the AIP in order to maximize the utilization of the 20%
DF and to achieve the objective of the fund which is to provide the constituents
the desirable socio-economic and environmental benefits that can be derived
from the completed projects.

7.7 We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat and the
MGS Officer strictly comply with the requirements of COA Circular Nos.
2009-001 and 96-010 in the submission of Contracts and Purchase Orders with
complete supporting documents to the Audit Team within the prescribed
period of five working days from their issuance for evaluation of procurement
transactions.
7.8 We also recommended that the MGS Officer inform the Audit Team within 24
hours the time and date of the scheduled delivery of the procured goods and
services as required in COA Circular No. 95-006 or thru the submission of the
Inspection and Acceptance Report to confirm propriety, validity and existence
of the items prior to payment.

8.9 We recommended that the BAC Chairperson, through the Secretariat shall
post in the PhilGEPs website, website of the Procuring Entity if available, and
in conspicuous place reserved for the purpose at the premises of the Procuring
Entity all Request for Quotations on procurements under Shopping and Small
Value Procurements with ABCs of more than P50,000.00.
Likewise, NOAs, Contracts/Purchase Orders & NTP shall be posted in the
PhilGEPs website, website of the Procuring Entity if available, if necessary
for information purposes, on procurements made thru the alternative methods
with amounts of more than P50,000.00 within 10 days from date of their
issuance as provided under Annex H of the 2016 RIRR of R.A. 9184; and
8.10 We also recommended for the BAC Secretariat to religiously comply with the
documentary requirements for the respective alternative methods of
procurement being resorted to under Appendix A of Annex H of the 2016
RIRR of R.A. 9184 for the Audit Team to confirm the validity and propriety
of the undertaken procurement transactions.

9.15 We recommended that the Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal HRMO, submit the DTRs of all permanent and casual agency
health workers and DTRs and Accomplishment Reports of all Job Order
health workers who were granted and paid with AHDP and SRA. This is
without prejudice to the issuance of Notice(s) of Suspension, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and government officials and employees concerned refund the excess amount of P89,377.25, the add-on fees for meals of P11,606.00 and the excessive hotel accommodation and meals of P20,492.00 during the ELA conducted in Taguig City.

We also recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Mayor evaluate the reasonableness of the costs which will be incurred by the Municipality for out-of-town LGU activities and exercise prudence in the use of government funds to avoid disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction:
The Management sent a letter-reply to the AOM justifying for the excessive disbursement and hoping for a reconsideration.




Evaluation on the reasonableness of expenditures made by the Municipality has been made by the Accountant prior to approval of the disbursements

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and officials/employees concerned to refund the amount of P494,000.00 immediately.  We also recommended that utilization of the 20% EDF funds be made only for expenditures expressly stated in the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 to avoid disallowance of transactions in audit.
observations.managementAction: A letter was submitted by Management stating therein their explanations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the General Services Officer conduct a physical count of all inventory items within 30 days from receipt of the Audit Report and to prepare and submit the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation purposes.  Thereafter, conduct the annual count of all inventory items of the Municipality.
We also recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist, General Services Officer and other officials concerned, prepare a certification that the items procured in CY 2015 and prior years, were already distributed to the identified beneficiaries and/or were used pursuant to the programs/projects of the Municipality, based on the RPCI prepared by the GSO and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant as his basis for the reduction of the Inventory account balance in the books of account of the Municipality.  Thereafter, the property/supply officer shall prepare the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) for every distribution/issuance of the stocks to the end users, summarize all RIS in the monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued and submit the report to the Municipal Accountant for recording purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to record the disposal of unserviceable properties and recognize the gain from sale of the assets in conformity with IPSAS 1 and 17 to present fairly the balances of the Property, Plant and Equipment, and the related Accumulated Depreciation accounts, and Government Equity in the financial statements of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to correct the erroneous recording of the items in the PPE account and its related Accumulated Depreciation and recognize the appropriate expense accounts for the issued items to the end users; and (ii) recognize properties/assets to the PPE account only when the criteria set in COA Circular No. 2020-006 has been fully met.

We recommended that the Mayor issue a directive to all the Department Heads, who are in-charge in the implementation of programs/projects of National Government Agencies, to:
Complete the implementation of the delayed/unfinished programs and projects;
Submit Purchase Requests and/or Program of Works promptly to the Municipal Bids and Awards Committee to commence the procurement process and ensure completion of the projects within the year.
Conduct regular meetings with officials of the Municipality to monitor and evaluate the completion of the projects and address the difficulties/problems encountered during its implementation, otherwise, return the fund to the Source Agency if the feasibility of the continuance of the projects are not viable.

We recommended that the Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal Development Council, and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, identify and include in the AIP priority projects which are ready for implementation in the current budget year and monitor its implementation to ensure optimal utilization of the 20% DF.
We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer:
Conduct a detailed engineering of all projects prior to its inclusion in the AIP which includes the preparation of the Program of Work with the Detailed Estimates and plans/specifications;
Fast-track the implementation of priority projects identified for CY 2020 and in the subsequent years; and
Submit to the Bids and Awards Committee the Program of Work with the Detailed Estimates, plans/specifications and Purchase Request within the timetable indicated in the AIP, taking into consideration the period within which the procurement process and project implementation will be completed.
We further recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee schedule the procurement of projects in accordance with the timeframe specified in the AIP to ensure completion of the projects before the end of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the LDRRM Council, through the MDRRM Officer:
Utilize the 30% Quick Response Fund for disaster response activities during state of calamities and not only reserve it for typhoon-response related activities;
Use the current year’s 5% LDRRM Fund in accordance with the approved MDRRM Investment Plan to ensure full utilization of the fund; and
Monitor and evaluate the Programs/Projects/Activities incorporated in the MDRRM Plan so as to avoid duplication of programs and identify projects that can address the current state of calamity or any calamity that may possibly arise.  Revise the Plan, if necessary and indicate specific timeframe of implementation.

We recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Mayor, through the Budget Officer, prepare Supplemental Budgets and the Local Sanggunian enact Appropriation Ordinances authorizing the Supplemental Budgets sourced from realigned funds especially during state of calamities and to disburse public funds strictly in accord with the approved appropriation of the Municipality to avoid disallowance of the transactions in audit.

We recommended that the Accountant create a Special Account in the General Fund for the BGCM Fund pursuant to LBC No. 125, to enable the easy identification of the expenditures from the fund, availability of records and monitoring and evaluation of the utilization of the funds.

We recommended that the Mayor, as the one who approves the disbursements and certifies on the legality and validity of the expenditures, and the Budget Officer, who certifies the existence of available appropriation, ensure that henceforth, all disbursements of the Municipality have the necessary Appropriation Ordinance especially those allowing the use of savings to fund new/other expenditures of the Municipality, prior to its obligation, to avoid disallowance of the disbursements in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries on the erroneous recording of receivables from Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax and the corresponding Deferred Credits account, including prior years.  Henceforth, set-up of receivables at the start of the year should exclude non-taxable properties.
observations.managementAction: Proper adjusting entry was made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record succeeding fines and penalties under the Tax Revenue – Fines and Penalties – Property Taxes account instead of deducting the amount from Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables and present a reliable balance in the books of the Municipality.  

Also, prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the amount of P898,705.00 as adjustment of the RPT/SEF receivables and the corresponding Deferred Credits account.
observations.managementAction: Proper adjusting entry was made and subsequent similar transactions were recorded based on the Audit Recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the cost of registration fees for seminar/conference/convention to the “Training Expense” account instead of the “Traveling Expense” account pursuant to The Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units.
observations.managementAction: The charging in the budget were also changed to conform to the AOM, consequently, the accounting entry was properly made in conformity with IPSAS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant monitor diligently the transfers of cash from the Special Education Fund/Trust Fund to the General Fund for taxes remitted from the GF to provide a complete and faithful representation of the transactions of the Municipality in the financial statements.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entry to record the cash transferred from the SEF/Trust Fund to the General Fund amounting to P424,010.46 representing the taxes withheld in November 2018 which were already remitted using the GF cash.
observations.managementAction:
Monitoring of the cash transferred from the TF/SEF to the GF has been regularly conducted.




Proper fund transfer was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Chairperson of the Bids and Awards Committee strictly comply with all the relevant provisions of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184 to ensure that transparency and competitiveness in all its procurement activities will be achieved and to avoid irregular disbursements.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat stated that the Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed are already posted in the PhilGEPS website.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, identify and implement as priority projects only those which are expressly allowed in Section 3.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: Management adheres strictly to DILG-DBM JMC 2017-1, together with the new circular in response to COVID Pandemic.

The Municipal Treasurer stated during the exit conference that she has already started preparing subsidiary records for the General Fund bank account and will monitor the actual cash received and disbursements made from the 20% Development Fund.




Adjustments in the Cash in Bank Account under the Special Account – 20% Development Fund were made in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Agriculturist re-evaluate the viability and location of the Diversion Dam project and revise its plans/design, where necessary and submit to DA RFO8 for approval to guarantee the immediate, continuous and full implementation of the project.
observations.managementAction: A re-evaluation was conducted and  changes in the location of the project site were already approved.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor make representation with the DSWD officials to determine the necessity in pursuing the unimplemented construction of Transitional Shelter in Brgy. San Ignacio, and whether the objective of the program can still be attained, considering the length of time that elapse from the occurrence of the earthquake.

If the source agency and the LGU decides to pursue the project, we reiterated our previous recommendation that the Mayor, MSWD Officer and other LGU officials immediately identify and acquire the lots for the relocation sites of Brgy. San Ignacio and construct the Transitional Shelters.
observations.managementAction:
Consultation with the officials of the Source Agency was made.




The Municipal Engineer and MSWD Officer stated that the resettlement lot was already purchased by Brgy. San Ignacio.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the MSWD Officer: 

Impose the liquidated damages from the supplier concerned due to late deliveries of the procured goods and ensure collection of the same.

Enforce compliance by the supplier of its obligation on the contract for the undelivered materials, or take other legal measures which will be most beneficial to the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
Liquidated damages were imposed to the delivered items which the supplier has paid.




Rescission of the contract were done.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the MSWDO and the Daycare Workers concerned maintain the database of the children-beneficiaries of the SFP, prepare the required nutritional status reports and submit the same on the prescribed dates, to facilitate the monitoring and evaluation of the nutritional status of the children-beneficiaries, pursuant to the relevant provisions of DSWD MC No. 03 series of 2019.

Also, we recommended that the MSWDO submit the Fund Utilization Report to the Municipal Accountant and COA every last Friday of the month to ensure subsequent release of funds and continuity of the Program.
observations.managementAction:
Nutritional status report was prepared and was part of the attachments in the liquidation to DSWD.




A Fund Utilization Report was submitted to DSWD-RO8 with the refund of the unutilized balance.

observations.recommendation:
To better ensure the effectiveness of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan of the Municipality, we reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the LDRRM Council, through the MDRRM Officer:

observations.managementAction: Establishment of MRFs was given priority by the LGU.
observations.reason: Not all barangays or clusters of barangays have existing MRFs

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and the designated MENRO ensure the continued implementation of the LGU's Solid Waste Management Plan through the following: adopt a viable and effective collection schedule for specific types of wastes to be collected; acquire additional garbage trucks to implement waste segregation at source and point of collection; widen the coverage of the municipality's garbage collection system; provide personal protective equipment and training on the proper handling of wastes to all collectors/personnel handling solid wastes to ensure their safety during garbage collection.
observations.managementAction: Collection and segregation of wastes was adopted by the LGU.
observations.reason: Waste segregation and collection were not made in all barangays of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Chairman, through the BAC Secretariat, to prepare the Annual Procurement Plan of the LGU for her approval to serve as guide of the municipality in their procurement activities.
observations.managementAction: The PPMP was consolidated to an APP by the Municipal Budget Officer in previous years. In CY 2017, this was consolidated by the BAC Secretariat.

observations.recommendation: We recommend: 1.) The Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory Committee and require the conduct of physical count of Inventories and PPE; 2.) The Municipal Treasurer and the Accounting Office hasten the updating of their records to facilitate the reconciliation on of the accounting and property records; 3.) The Municipal Accountant should review the details of properties which are non-functional or unserviceable and prepare the necessary adjustments, if warranted; 4.) Require the committee to prepare a methodical and well planned physical inventory taking, which could be done in a year round basis, until all the Property, Plant and Equipment of the municipality have been accounted for and listed in the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officer prepared and submitted an RPCPPE.
observations.reason: Not all reconciliations between the RPCPPE and accounting records were conducted.  Also, the RPCPPE was made per office and not by kind/type of PPE to facilitate reconciliation of records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: Require all concerned officials, responsible in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to strictly observe the rules and regulations provided in COA Circular 97-002 to avoid accumulation of cash advances. Institute measures by notifying the persons concerned to enforce settlement of the unliquidated cash advance or resort to withholding of salaries pursuant to Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002. For deceased personnel, please submit relevant documents to provide basis for its write off from the books. For employees of the Bureau of Fire and COMELEC, the Local Chief Executive must issue a demand letter to concerned employees through their respective head of agency.
observations.managementAction: Demand Letters were sent to the accountable officers.
observations.reason: The amount of P60,000.00 was collected by the LGU, however the remaining balance remained unliquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to remit all deductions due to the GSIS intact and verify the subsidiary accounts of the Due to GSIS to correct the negative balances and the unremitted balance carried through the years. It is mandatory that only those employees with deduction are included in the remittance list.
observations.managementAction: Premium payments and loan amortizations were remitted to GSIS.
observations.reason: Remittances made by the LGU were equal to the monthly billing made by the GSIS. However, an observation on under deduction of premium contributions (personal share) and loan amortizations was noted and included in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should make a written confirmation to Land Bank of the Philippines on the nature of the deposit so it can be recorded accordingly.
observations.managementAction: The amount was recorded in the books of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the submission of the required PFP Disposition and Allocation Form every quarter and other reports needed for monitoring and evaluation of the program relative to the releases of PFPR/Capitation Fund. This is necessary to evaluate the effectiveness and efficiency of the PCB 1 package, and to determine if the disbursements conformed to government rules and regulations, policies and Implementing Guidelines of PCB 1.
observations.managementAction: Quarterly Reports are submitted to Philhealth.
observations.reason: No reports were submitted to the team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer prepare the required RAAO and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit monthly the Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) pursuant to Section 5.1.4 and 5.1.5 COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Quarterly reports were prepared by the Accountant.
observations.reason: Monthly reports were not prepared and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management prepare an inventory report of all insurable properties of the municipality and to submit to the General Insurance Fund (GIF) of the Government Service Insurance System for proper processing of the insurance coverage of all insurable properties so that in case of loss, destruction and or damages, the cost of these assets will be recovered. Moreover, management should include/provide funds in the annual budget for this purpose.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has already initiated the inclusion of LGU properties with the GSIS Insurance coverage.
observations.reason: No insurance coverage of all LGU properties with GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, the HRMO and the Municipal Accountant: Attach/Submit the duly certified copy of the Job Order Contracts/Service Contracts by the HRMO; Require each payee to prepare the accomplishment report duly verified and accepted by the immediate supervisor; Fill up the existing vacant plantilla positions which are vital in the delivery of basic services which would lessen the number of hired job order employees.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officer prepared and submitted accomplishment reports were attached to the payrolls.
observations.reason: Service contracts were not attached to the disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should require the Municipal Accountant to exert efforts to locate documents to substantiate the recorded balances, to locate these debtors and issue demand letters. After all efforts exerted ran to naught, concerned management officials could request from the Commission on Audit (COA) for write-off of the dormant accounts following prescribed procedures.
observations.managementAction: List of Debtors under the Plant Now Pay Later program was presented by the Municipal Agriculturist.
observations.reason: No Request for Write-Off was submitted to the audit team duly supported by the demand letters.

observations.recommendation: To avoid the presumptions of intentional omissions or neglect in adhering to lawful rules, we recommend management to strictly comply with the required report preparations involving fuel consumptions and to abide with R.A. 9184 in the procurement of Fuel.
observations.managementAction: Monthly report for fuel consumption was prepared by the LGU.
observations.reason: Purchase of fuel was not made in bulk.

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommend that Driver’s Trip tickets should be prepared for each vehicle per travel. It should be serially numbered, properly and completely accomplished, duly authorized and approved by the Chief of Office or his authorized representative. Places visited and the distance traveled in kilometers should be clearly stated. A Monthly Report of Official Travels (MROT) shall be prepared by the driver concerned summarizing in chronological order his trips for the month. In addition, a Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption shall be prepared and submitted by the Agency personnel concerned to the Auditor for verification purposes.
observations.managementAction: Trip tickets were prepared.
observations.reason: Trip tickets were not completely and properly filled up.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for strict adherence to the practice of checking as to completeness of documentations before claims shall be paid. Require all suppliers of medicines to submit necessary documentations as mandated under Section 9.1.3.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to ensure that procured items are from legitimate sources to guarantee its effectiveness and genuineness.
observations.managementAction: Documents were attached to support the purchase of drugs and medicines.
observations.reason: Certain documents such as certification by the requisitioning officer that the procured items fall within and conform with the PNDF Vol.1 and the batch release certificate from the FDA.

observations.recommendation: To correct the deficiencies, we recommend to conduct planning and evaluation of the affairs and condition of the Rural Health Unit of the Municipality and the needs of its constituents. Along this line, implement the projects intended of the fund in order to attain its objectives of providing basic health services and facilities as mandated by law.
observations.managementAction: PPAs were included in the budget using the Philhealth Capitation Fund.
observations.reason: The Philhealth Capitation Fund was still not fully utilized and has a balance of P7,169,429.68 as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management ensure that the gender responsive program/activity/project provided with appropriation be implemented in order to maximize the utilization of the same. Moreover, require the GAD Focal Point/Person to submit the agency's GAD Plan to the DBM and NCRFW for review and approval to ensure that the programs/projects/activities reflected thereat are in accordance with the existing regulations and guidelines. Likewise, the GAD Focal Point/Person shall furnish the NCRFW, COA and other regulating offices the GAD Plan & Budget and Accomplishment Reports for monitoring purposes. Management should also monitor and guarantee the establishment of VAW Desk in every barangay within their respective jurisdiction as required under RA 9710.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Plan and Budget was prepared and Accomplishment Reports were submitted.
observations.reason: Utilization of the allocation for GAD was not maximized since not all the PPAs included in the plan was accomplished by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: To achieve on-time submission of accounts, we recommend that the Treasurer and the Accountant be directed to promptly turn-over documents, reports and journals under their possession on the prescribed time which shall be duly covered by transmittal letters; in turn, cause their prompt submission for audit. Said transmittal letters shall prove the compliance of each to their duties over the agency's accounts.
observations.managementAction: Monthly reports were submitted to the audit team.
observations.reason: Submission of monthly reports and accounts were still delayed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the List of Taxpayers
showing the amount due and collectible and submit the same to the Municipal
Accountant as basis in recording the receivable.

Also, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor initiate legal actions for the
collection of unpaid taxes to ensure that there are sufficient funds for the
maintenance and operations of public schools in the municipality.

We recommend that the Mayor require the concerned officials who
authorized the issuance of the inventory items to prioritize the submission of the
Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) before the end of CY 2017 and mandate the
General Services Officer to consolidate all the RIS and submit the same to the
Accountant as basis in removing the cost of such items in the Inventory accounts.
Also, we recommend that the Mayor instruct the General Services Officer
and all personnel with custody of supplies and materials for distribution to issue the
same only when the RIS has been duly prepared and approved by the proper
officials.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor together with the MPDO,
Municipal Engineer and other Heads of Implementing Offices, plan adequately and
thoroughly all infrastructure projects prior to its inclusion in the Annual
Investment Plan to ensure its immediate implementation and completion within the
timeframe set by management, and guarantee the full utilization of the 20%
Development Fund thereby attaining continuous development of the municipality.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Engineer/Project Coordinators and other personnel involved in the
implementation of projects to:
1.) secure a copy of the Memorandum of Agreement from the source agencies
including the Work and Financial Plan to determine the period of project
implementation and other conditions of the funds transferred;
2.) conduct adequate planning to ensure proper scheduling in the
implementation of all LGU projects taking into consideration the available
resources of the LGU;
3.) implement all projects within the timeframe agreed upon.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with
the project coordinators, refund the unexpended balances of funds for
projects/programs which have been completed, to the Source Agencies or request
the transfer thereof to the General Fund of the LGU, if the disposition of the
unexpended balances is not provided in the MOA.

We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management
Council through the MDRRRMO, replace the ineligible projects with disaster-
related programs/projects in order to maximize the fund in accordance with the
purpose. Also, include in the LDRRMFIP only projects and activities that are
allowed under NDRRMC, DBM & DILG JMC No. 2013-1 to ensure that the
purpose for which the LDRRMF was created will be achieved.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Disaster
Risk Reduction Management Officer consider all aspects (time table, people,
workload, weather and others) in planning the implementation of projects,
programs and activities to ensure that said PPAs will be implemented within the
year and the 70% Mitigation Fund of the LDRRMF are fully utilized in accordance
with the purpose.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and MDRRMO include in the
annual MDRRM Plan, programs, projects or activities to be funded from the cash
donations amounting P2.4M to address the post recovery and rehabilitation
needs of the typhoon victims.

We recommend that the Local School Board, through the Municipal Mayor
as Chairman, include only in the Special Education Fund budget those projects,
programs and activities which are authorized to be incurred therefrom. Also,
utilize the SEF only for activities for which the SEF is intended.

We recommend that the Mayor create a Local Council for the Protection of
Children and require the said Council to develop and identify the programs,
projects and activities to be implemented by the LGU using the 1% allocated from
its annual IRA pursuant to RA 9344.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal
Solid Waste Management Board, submit the additional requirements to the
NSWMC to ensure the full approval of the LGUs Solid Waste Management Plan
for CY 2015-2025 in its entirety.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and the designated Municipal
Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO), in collaboration with the
Punong Barangays, prioritize the establishment of MRFs in all barangays or in
clustered barangays, and utilize the facility to the fullest to promote segregation of
wastes, recycling of materials, reuse of resources, and reduction of solid waste.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and the designated Municipal
Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) ensure the continued
implementation of the LGUs Solid Waste Management Plan through the
following:
1) adopt a viable and effective collection schedule for specific types of wastes
to be collected;
2) acquire additional garbage trucks to implement waste segregation at
source and point of collection;
3) widen the coverage of the municipalitys garbage collection system;
4) provide personal protective equipment and training on the proper
handling of wastes to all collectors/personnel handling solid wastes to
ensure their safety during garbage collection

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and MENRO prioritize the
development of a sanitary landfill in accordance with the provisions of RA 9003.
Also, adopt measures to prevent or at least lessen the risks to the public and the
environment brought about by the operation of the existing open dumpsite of the
LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Chairman,
through the BAC Secretariat, to prepare the Annual Procurement Plan of the LGU
for her approval to serve as guide of the municipality in their procurement
activities.

observations.reason: Only a partial inventory was conducted.
Some items purchased in CY 2016 were already recorded as expense but remained unadjusted.
The Municipal officials exert earnest efforts to demand the liquidation of cash advances. Some amounts reversed in CY 2016.
Management reversed some unliquidated advances and took actions to collect others; still some remain unliquidated as of Dec 31, 2016.
Management verified subsidiary ledger and remittances for succeeding years; only those with deduction are included in remittance list.
JEV was prepared by the Municipal Accountant to revert the effect of the stale check to the cash in bank account.
The Municipal Accountant communicated to the bank regarding the unrecorded amount; not yet recorded in the books.
No copies of the required PFPR from Philhealth were submitted; needed for monitoring and evaluation.
Cash refunds were made by the payees; refund involved those covered by honoraria only.
The 20% Development Fund was no longer used for the purchase of office equipment and furnitures and fixtures and IT equipment; purchases were responsive to needs.
Shares of barangay proceeds were remitted.
Municipal Assessor made a written communication to the LCE for titling; process ongoing.
Financial statements for each economic enterprise prepared and consolidated into General Fund.
The PPAs are identified and the RAAO and required reports prepared; however monthly/quarterly reports not submitted for CY 2016 and accountant instead submitted monthly reports.
Common-use supplies were procured in accordance with RA 9184; procurement of common supplies adhered to regulations.
LGU made inquiries to GSIS on how to insure their properties; assessment ongoing which properties qualify.
Accomplishment reports were not attached to payroll; HRMO required to submit; remedial actions suggested.
Utilization using the funds was made; liquidations prepared.
Some projects implemented; similar observation contained in Part II No. 4 of this report.
Some implementation of projects funded from the 20% DF undertaken; recommendation to prioritize projects in AIP.
Agriculturist presented list of debtors and demand letters were issued; some documents furnished to audit team.
A monthly report for fuel consumption was prepared by the LGU; practice of purchasing fuel in bulk not adopted.
Trip tickets/driver reports were prepared for CY 2016 but not properly serially numbered and fully detailed; monthly reports to be prepared.
Documents attached to support purchase of medicines were lacking; measures recommended to ensure suppliers provide required certifications.
Practice of deducting personal loan amortizations from salaries stopped; LGU refrained from agreements with private lending institutions.
PPAs identified and incorporated in APP; however some projects not implemented as of Dec 31,2016; capitation fund still has balance.
No documents were submitted; AREs/acknowledgement receipts not included.
Assessor submitted records to Treasurer for Real Property Tax Account; recommendation to adopt iTAX to improve records and collections.
Treasurer issued demand letters to delinquent taxpayers and took steps for collection; further remedies available under Local Government Code.
GAD programs and projects were mainstreamed; no GAD Plan submitted for CY 2016.
Local Energy Board passed resolution; LGU implemented Projects for CY 2016 reducing electricity costs.
Planned projects under the 20% DF were refrained from being used for purposes other than those enumerated in the implementing guidelines.
Project inspected and supporting documents provided; variations justified; some items suspended.
Treasurer and Accountant directed to promptly turn-over documents; accounts submission still delayed.
The Local Solid Waste Management Board was created and the SWM Plan prepared; similar observation in Part II No. 10.
